title: carex atherodes var. longilanceolata
intro: Carex atherodes var. longilanceolata, also known as long-leaf sedge, is a type of grass-like perennial plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The long-leaf sedge is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Carex atherodes var. longilanceolata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
